headline: Government Launches 380 Apps With Most Having Low Adoption
context: Malaysian government agencies launched 380 mobile apps but 72% show fewer than 10,000 active users.

cards.big: 380 government apps launched but 72% have under 10,000 active users
cards.sub: Fragmented app ecosystem wastes public funds and confuses citizens

cards.big: Total spending on government apps reached RM280 million since 2019
cards.sub: Average cost per app RM737,000 including development and maintenance

cards.big: MySejahtera most downloaded at 42 million but active users dropped 85%
cards.sub: Government spent RM70 million to repurpose as general health platform

cards.big: MAMPU proposed consolidating 380 apps into 12 super-apps by domain
cards.sub: Agency resistance protecting budget allocations has blocked consolidation

cards.big: App proliferation tests whether government digital strategy prioritizes user needs or agency turf
380 government apps with low adoption. Fewer, better apps serve citizens; proliferation serves bureaucratic silos.
